diseases.name: Malaria
diseases.description: A life-threatening disease caused by parasites transmitted to people through the bites of infected female Anopheles mosquitoes.

diseases.name: Typhoid Fever
diseases.description: A bacterial infection caused by Salmonella typhi, usually spread through contaminated food or water.

diseases.name: Common Cold
diseases.description: A viral infection of your nose and throat (upper respiratory tract). It's usually harmless.

diseases.name: Influenza (Flu)
diseases.description: A common viral infection that can be deadly, especially in high-risk groups, affecting lungs, nose, and throat.

diseases.name: Food Poisoning
diseases.description: Illness caused by food contaminated with bacteria, viruses, parasites, or toxins.

diseases.name: COVID-19
diseases.description: An infectious respiratory illness caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus, with highly variable symptoms.

diseases.name: Pneumonia
diseases.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs, which may fill with fluid or pus.

diseases.name: Urinary Tract Infection (UTI)
diseases.description: An infection in any part of the urinary system, including kidneys, bladder, or urethra.

diseases.name: Migraine
diseases.description: A neurological condition that can cause multiple symptoms, preeminently intense throbbing headaches.

diseases.name: Dehydration
diseases.description: A harmful reduction in the amount of water in the body, caused by losing more fluid than you take in.

diseases.name: Gastroenteritis
diseases.description: An intestinal infection marked by watery diarrhea, abdominal cramps, nausea or vomiting, and sometimes fever.

diseases.name: Asthma
diseases.description: A condition in which your airways narrow and swell and may produce extra mucus, making breathing difficult.
